summary.intent: Perform a CEO-level and engineering-level plan review for CHA-17 (P1.10: RPC methods crm.matters.*), identifying scope, architecture gaps, code quality issues, test coverage, and performance concerns before implementation begins.
summary.outcome: A comprehensive engineering review plan was produced and committed to the repository at plans/2026-03-17-plan-crm-matters-rpc-methods.md, documenting 14 issues across architecture, code quality, tests, and performance, along with a test plan artifact. No implementation code was written.

repo: The CRM stack follows a strict layered pattern: SQLite store → CrmStore trait → CrmService trait (service-traits crate) → LiveCrmService → RPC handler registration in methods/services.rs → WebSocket dispatch in ws.rs

Both crm.contacts.list and crm.matters.list call unfiltered list methods despite the store layer having richer filtered variants (list_matters_filtered, list_filtered) — a consistent gap across the CRM API surface
The store already has list_matters_filtered(contact_id, practice_area), list_matters_by_contact(contact_id), but neither is wired to the service trait or RPC layer
Contacts have much richer API patterns (list_filtered with stage/search/offset/limit, get_with_channels composite query, get_by_external lookup) that matters lack entirely
The memory crate uses FTS5 for chunk search — the pattern exists in the repo for full-text search if matters volume grows
now_ms() is duplicated in crates/crm/src/types.rs and crates/gateway/src/crm_service.rs — a known minor DRY violation
Dynamic SQL WHERE clauses are built via string concatenation with bound ? params — the established pattern, no query builder in use
DB indexes exist on crm_matters for updated_at, contact_id, and status, but NOT on phase or practice_area

code.finding: list_matters() and list_matters_filtered() are separate implementations; list_matters() should delegate to list_matters_filtered to eliminate duplication
list_matters_filtered only accepts contact_id and practice_area; missing status, phase, search, offset, limit parameters
list_matters_by_contact(contact_id) is redundant with list_matters_filtered(Some(contact_id), None) — candidate for deprecation
parse_matter requires id and title but doesn't validate id looks like a UUID — same gap as all other CRM entity parsers
now_ms() duplicated here and in crates/crm/src/types.rs:607

workflow: The autonomous agent workflow uses a Linear comment as a persistent workpad, updated at each milestone with status/decisions/next-steps
The eng-review skill produces a structured four-section review (Architecture, Code Quality, Tests, Performance) before any implementation begins
Scope challenge (Step 0) explicitly asks whether existing code already solves each sub-problem and counts files-to-touch as a complexity proxy (threshold: 8 files)
The agent writes a test plan artifact to ~/.gstack/projects/ in addition to committing the plan to the repo
context-mode MCP tool had path resolution issues during the session; direct file tools (Read, Glob, Grep) were more reliable

friction: context-mode MCP tool (ctx_batch_execute) failed with path issues, requiring fallback to direct Read/Glob/Grep tools
Linear issue URL is behind auth — the agent could not fetch issue details directly and had to infer requirements from code exploration alone
The CEO-review agent called AskUserQuestion (violating the no-human-in-the-loop ground rule), causing a session interruption that required manual continuation
open_items: now_ms() duplication between crates/crm/src/types.rs and crates/gateway/src/crm_service.rs — deferred as too minor for this PR
UUID validation in parse_matter (and all other CRM entity parsers) — deferred because fixing matters alone would be inconsistent; needs a cross-entity cleanup
crm.contacts.list has the same no-filtering gap as crm.matters.list — not in scope for CHA-17 but should be addressed for API consistency
FTS5 full-text search for matters — LIKE-based search accepted for now; FTS5 noted as future option if matter volumes grow
14 new tests identified as required (T1-T14) but not yet written — to be implemented in the coding phase
Missing DB indexes on phase and practice_area columns — requires a new migration to be written

Backward-compatibility decision for list_matters pagination defaults is unresolved: final approach is to only apply limit=50 default when any filter param is present, preserving all-records behavior for parameterless calls
